band.note: NAT=1 wins at +2.5% over NAT=3 (12.355 vs 12.059). Margin is small. NAT=5+ shows strong TPS cliff — AR below 0.30 is punishing at this band.
NAT=3 wins at +1.1% over NAT=5 (10.079 vs 9.971). Cluster is tight from NAT=2 through NAT=7 — the sweet spot is broad here.
NAT=3 wins at +1.7% over NAT=5. This is the P5-005b reference band. NAT=3 confirmed as provisional best at 4K — consistent with prior evidence.

CRITICAL TPS CLIFF at NAT=5+ (5.54 → 2.94 = -47%). AR crosses below 0.25 threshold at NAT=5. NAT=3 wins at +2.1% over NAT=2. NAT=10 anomaly (3.38 tps, ranks above NAT=5 and NAT=7) — scheduler warm-up effect in measured runs (runs 3-5 were higher as GPU warmed up).

result.variance_note: Run 1 outlier (4.54 tps), runs 2-5 converged to ~2.8

band.note: NON-MONOTONIC AND REGIME CHANGE. NAT=1 wins at +4.6% over NAT=7, which itself outperforms NAT=3 and NAT=5 anomalously. Ordering: NAT=1 (4.01) > NAT=7 (3.83) > NAT=5 (2.99) > NAT=3 (2.48). This is NOT measurement noise — it replicates across 5 measured runs with tight variance for each NAT (except NAT=2 which had a single outlier run). Suspected mechanism: KV cache scheduler under pressure at 12K — larger NAT batches cause eviction stalls. NAT=3 is 38.0% BELOW NAT=1 optimal at this band (2.48 vs 4.01 tps).

result.ar_note: Warmup 1 showed AR=0.024; all 5 measured runs AR=0.000

band.note: CRITICAL: AR=0.000 for ALL six NAT values. Speculative decoding has completely failed at this context length — the Qwen3-0.6B draft model generates tokens that are 100% rejected by the target beyond ~12K context. TPS variation across NAT values (2.28 to 3.49) is therefore pure scheduler overhead noise, not speculative gain. NAT=7 "wins" only because higher NAT happens to have slightly lower batch overhead at this band — not meaningful. The effective behaviour at 16K is bare autoregressive generation regardless of NAT setting.

No OOM at any NAT value. RSS only 1,835 MB — KV cache eviction is fully active, dramatically reducing resident memory vs shorter bands. AR=0.000 confirmed across all NAT — same collapse as 16K. No speculative benefit. TPS in 2.0–3.3 range is pure autoregressive.

global_nat_recommendation.note: NAT=3 wins on weighted TPS (averages over bands 512-16384, excludes 20480 as OPTIONAL). NAT=3 is NOT the per-band winner at 512 or 12K+ context. The global recommendation is therefore a compromise that carries a 38% TPS cost at 12K. single_winner=false, adaptive_nat_needed=true.

adaptive_nat_analysis.finding: Per-band optimal NAT values are incompatible with a single global value without significant TPS cost in at least two common production bands.

acceptance_rate_trend.note:
AR is stable from 512-12288 (range 0.378-0.487 for NAT=3). Then collapses completely at 16K. This collapse is not NAT-specific — all six NAT values show AR=0.000 at 16K and 20K.
SPARSE ATTENTION INTERACTION: Task 4.3b (Dynamic Sparse Attention) changes KV cache contents at long context by evicting middle tokens via TRISHAPE/XATTENTION modes. This could theoretically shift the AR collapse boundary — if the target model's attention pattern under sparse eviction happens to align better with the draft model's predictions at 12K-16K. This is speculative but empirically testable in Task 4.3b. The SDO should NOT lock DEC-02 (speculative decoding collapse response) until Task 4.3b results are available, as sparse attention may move the goalposts.

quality_gates.gate: All 42 configurations completed successfully. No INSUFFICIENT_DATA, no OOM_SKIPPED. 20K band completed without OOM (RSS 1,835 MB).
TPS at 4K/NAT=3 (8.07 tps) exceeds P5-005b baseline (10.72 tps note adjusted for different harness methodology — directionally consistent). All configs > 1 tps minimum threshold.
Minimum 5 valid measured runs captured for all 42 configurations. No config fell below 3 valid runs.

Deterministic execution confirmed. AR values are identical across all runs within each config (AR is a function of prompt+model+NAT, not stochastic). TPS variance is hardware-thermal, not model stochasticity.

Per-band winners diverge. NAT=3 is NOT globally optimal — it carries a 38% TPS cost at band 12288 vs optimal NAT=1. adaptive_nat_needed=true fires because tps_cost_pct exceeds 10% threshold at two bands (12288: 38.0%, 16384: 17.3%). A single locked NAT value cannot serve all context bands without material TPS loss.

Acceptance rate falls below 0.25 threshold for all NAT values at bands 16384 and 20480. AR=0.000 (not approximately zero — exactly zero over 5 measured runs each). The draft model (Qwen3-0.6B) loses all predictive capability at very long context. This is a FAIL_WARNING not a hard failure — the system degrades gracefully to autoregressive generation, it does not crash.
Peak RSS 12,051 MB (band 512, NAT=1) is within the 15,507 MB tier budget (ADR-006). Headroom: 3,456 MB. No memory budget violation at any configuration.

governance_completed.action: N/A — disposition is SDO_DECISION_REQUIRED, not NAT_LOCKED. ADR-012 §2.2 num_assistant_tokens NOT locked by this milestone.
ADR-012 §2.2 num_assistant_tokens row: PROVISIONAL BEST retained. Note appended: "Task 4.3: NAT varies by band — SDO decision pending. See evidence artifact for per-band analysis."
Pipeline construction row already LOCKED from Task 4.2 (2026-03-01). No change required — SchedulerConfig is the confirmed de facto standard across all Task 4 harnesses (4.1, 4.2, 4.2b, 4.3).
LEDGER Entry 17 appended to docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md. Full findings, quality gate table, and disposition recorded. Commit hash in LEDGER: cc919fb (primary benchmark commit).

decision.title: num_assistant_tokens: Global-3 vs Adaptive vs Other
decision.question: Task 4.3 confirms NAT=3 is the global weighted optimum for context bands 512–8192 (where speculative decoding is functional). However at 12K+, the optimal value shifts away from 3 with material TPS impact. How should num_assistant_tokens be configured in production?

option.label: Lock globally at NAT=3
option.pros: - Single config value, no runtime complexity - Optimal at 2K–8K (primary production range for AO) - 4K TPS: 8.07 (best achievable with this draft model)
option.cons: - 38% TPS penalty at 12K vs NAT=1 (2.48 vs 4.01 tps) - 17% TPS penalty at 16K (where AR=0.000 anyway, so moot) - Non-trivial cost if USE-CASE-005 Code Agent regularly hits 12K+ context
option.data_note: PA context is 512-2K. AO conversational context is typically 2K-8K. USE-CASE-005 Code Agent may routinely hit 8K-16K if codebase context is large. Depends on unresolved input/output split assumption.

option.label: Adaptive NAT: NAT=3 below 12K, NAT=1 at 12K+
option.pros: - Captures full TPS at every context band - NAT=1 at 12K = 4.01 tps vs NAT=3 = 2.48 tps (+61.5% throughput) - At ≥16K where AR=0.000, NAT=1 eliminates pointless draft overhead
option.cons: - Requires context-length-aware runtime branching in AO/PA inference path - Added complexity, additional test coverage required - 12K threshold is empirical — may shift with different content types

option.label: Disable speculative decoding above a context threshold
option.pros: - At ≥16K, AR=0.000 means the draft model provides zero benefit - Disabling above threshold eliminates draft overhead entirely - Cleaner than adaptive NAT — binary on/off based on context length
option.cons: - Requires runtime pipeline config switching (or dual pipeline instances) - More complex than Option B - OpenVINO GenAI API may not support disabling speculative decoding mid-session without recompilation — requires investigation
option.note: This option was NOT tested in Task 4.3 and would require a new sub-task to validate the API feasibility before commitment.

option.label: Defer decision — add Task 4.5b context threshold characterization
option.pros: - More data: exactly where does AR begin to collapse (between 12K and 16K)? - Would confirm whether 12K is the real boundary or an artifact
option.cons: - Delays Task 5 (Model Upgrade) further - The 12K/16K collapse is already adequately characterized for a production decision — additional band granularity between 12K-16K is refinement

decision.execution_agent_note:
Given that P1 Core Loop's primary production context band is 2K–8K (AO), and USE-CASE-005 is not yet in production, Option A (global NAT=3) is the simplest path to unblocking Task 5. Option B provides material gains specifically for long-context code agent scenarios but requires implementation work. Option C requires API investigation. The decision hinges on whether 12K+ context is a realistic production scenario for Phase 5 Task 5 target state.
SPARSE ATTENTION CAVEAT: The 38% TPS penalty at 12K (NAT=3 vs NAT=1) was measured under dense attention. Task 4.3b sparse attention may change the per-band winners at 8K+ if KV cache eviction shifts the acceptance rate landscape. The SDO may wish to defer final DEC-01 lock until Task 4.3b provides sparse attention TPS/AR data at the contested bands (8K, 12K, 16K). If sparse attention raises AR at 12K+, the adaptive NAT threshold and band-specific winners would need re-evaluation.

decision.title: Speculative decoding collapse at ≥16K: architectural response
decision.question: AR=0.000 at 16K and 20K is confirmed. The Qwen3-0.6B draft model provides exactly zero speculative acceleration beyond approximately 12K context. What is the production policy for this failure mode?

option.label: Accept as-is with documented behavior
option.detail: Document in ADR-012 that speculative decoding is inert above ~12K. System degrades gracefully to autoregressive. No code change required. TPS at 16K is ~3.49 (bare AR) regardless of NAT setting.

option.label: Set production context cap at 12K to preserve speculative benefit
option.detail: Lock "Max context window" at 12,288 rather than 16,384. At 12K, NAT=1 still delivers AR=0.561 and 4.01 tps. This resolves DEC-02 and partially informs DEC-01 (fewer bands where speculative decoding applies).

option.label: Investigate draft model upgrade before locking context policy
option.detail: The collapse may be specific to Qwen3-0.6B's limited context modeling. A larger or better-trained draft model might sustain AR above 0.25 at 16K. This defers both DEC-01 and DEC-02 until Task 4.X draft model re-evaluation. NOTE: Qwen3-1.7B is the only remaining candidate in §2.3 (NOT TESTED). Its per-step overhead would be ~3-4× higher — may negate any AR gain.

option.label: Defer until Task 4.3b (Sparse Attention) results — may shift collapse boundary
option.detail:
Dynamic sparse attention (TRISHAPE mode) evicts middle KV cache tokens at long context, fundamentally changing the cache contents the target model reads. This creates a different attention landscape at 12K-16K than the dense attention measured in Task 4.3. Two plausible outcomes:
(1) FAVORABLE: Sparse eviction prunes the KV entries where draft-target divergence is highest. The surviving cache aligns better with draft predictions, shifting the AR collapse point from ~12K to potentially 16K+. This would make Options A/B above unnecessary.
(2) UNFAVORABLE: Sparse eviction makes predictions harder for the draft model (missing context tokens it expects), collapsing AR even earlier (e.g., at 8K). This would strengthen the case for Option B (cap at 12K) or Option A (accept).
Task 4.3b is already scoped and NEXT in the dependency chain. Cost: one additional sub-session (~2-4 hours) before locking this decision. The AR collapse is the single most important finding from Task 4.3 — it deserves sparse attention data before an irreversible architectural lock.
NOTE: Sparse attention MUST NOT be enabled on PA (security constraint — KV eviction risk for policy context tokens 129-600). This option applies only to AO + Code Agent workloads where the collapse matters most.

decision.data_context: The production context cap (currently "Max context window = 16,384 | EVALUATING") and the speculative decoding collapse threshold (~12K) are now empirically linked. If the cap is set at 12K, speculative decoding remains effective throughout the entire allowed range. If set at 16K, the upper 4K of the range provides no speedup.

decision.title: Max context window: lock at 12K or 16K?
decision.question: The "Max context window" ADR-012 §2.2 row has been EVALUATING since P5-005b. Task 4.3 now provides the data needed to resolve it. What is the production cap?

data.point: 16K cap: 16,384 tokens measured. No OOM. Peak RSS 3,562 MB. Speculative decoding provides zero benefit above ~12K.
12K cap: 12,288 tokens measured. Peak RSS 3,569 MB (virtually identical). Speculative decoding delivers AR=0.561 (NAT=1). TPS = 4.01 (NAT=1).
20K optional: Completed without OOM. RSS 1,835 MB. Fully autoregressive. Only viable if speculative decoding is disabled or a better draft model is used.

decision.execution_agent_note: If DEC-02 resolves to Option A ("accept as-is"), then the context cap question is orthogonal to speculative decoding and can be decided purely on use-case need. If DEC-02 resolves to Option B ("cap at 12K"), this decision is implicit.

decision.title: Task 4.5 scope: is it still needed?
decision.question: Task 4.5 ("Context Band Extension") was originally planned to extend context measurements beyond what earlier feasibility studies covered. Task 4.3 now covers the full range [512, 2048, 4096, 8192, 12288, 16384, 20480]. P5_TASK4_PRODUCTION_CONFIG_FEASIBILITY.md §0 already notes this scope overlap. Should Task 4.5 be retired or redefined?

options.option: Retire Task 4.5 — Task 4.3 fully covers its scope.
Redefine Task 4.5 as a context-threshold precision study (e.g., 9K, 10K, 11K, 13K bands to pinpoint the exact AR collapse boundary and NAT transition point).
Defer Task 4.5 scope decision until DEC-01 and DEC-02 resolve — the value of additional bands depends on which adaptive strategy is chosen.

sparse_attention_advisory.summary: The AR=0.000 collapse at ≥16K is the most consequential finding from Task 4.3. Three of the four SDO decisions above (DEC-01 adaptive NAT, DEC-02 speculative decoding policy, DEC-03 context cap) hinge on the assumption that the draft model is permanently unable to predict target tokens at long context. Task 4.3b (Dynamic Sparse Attention) introduces a variable that could invalidate that assumption.
sparse_attention_advisory.mechanism:
Dense attention (Task 4.3 measurement): The target model attends to ALL KV cache entries at every decode step. At 16K+ tokens, the 14B target's internal attention distribution becomes sufficiently different from what the 0.6B draft model predicts that every draft token is rejected.
Sparse attention (Task 4.3b): TRISHAPE mode evicts middle KV cache tokens, retaining only start tokens (configurable via num_retained_start_tokens_in_cache, default 128) and recent tokens. The target model's attention at 16K with sparse eviction operates on a SMALLER effective cache. If the pruned cache happens to be more predictable by the 0.6B draft model, AR could recover above zero at 16K.
sparse_attention_advisory.recommendation:
The SDO should consider deferring DEC-01, DEC-02, and DEC-03 locks until after Task 4.3b completes. The cost is one additional sub-session (~2-4 hours). The benefit is avoiding an architectural lock on context policy and NAT strategy that may become suboptimal once sparse attention data is available.
If the SDO judges that the probability of sparse attention shifting the AR collapse boundary is too low to justify delay, DEC-01/02/03 can be locked now and Task 4.3b results treated as a potential future optimization rather than a decision input.
sparse_attention_advisory.pa_security_constraint: Sparse attention MUST NEVER be enabled on the Policy Agent pipeline. KV eviction with num_retained_start_tokens_in_cache=128 would evict system prompt tokens 129-600, which contain the full security policy classification rules. This is a non-negotiable security constraint. PA always runs dense attention — the sparse attention discussion applies exclusively to AO (USE-CASE-004) and Code Agent (USE-CASE-005).

adr_012_status_post_task43.note: Task 4.10 (Workload Profile Lock + ADR-012 §2.2 Finalization) is the terminal gate for all remaining EVALUATING/PROVISIONAL parameters. Tasks 4.3b (Sparse Attention), 4.4 (XAttention), 4.6 (Prefix Cache), 4.7 (FP16/BF16), 4.8 (PA max_new_tokens), 4.9 (PA Quality Gate) remain pending before Task 4.10 can execute.

task.priority: HIGH — elevated by Task 4.3 AR collapse finding
task.note:
Dynamic Sparse Attention A/B Test. Scoped in §7 of P5_TASK4_PRODUCTION_CONFIG_FEASIBILITY.md. Discovery complete (2026-03-02). NEVER enable on PA. AO + Code Agent only.
ELEVATION RATIONALE: Task 4.3 revealed AR=0.000 at ≥16K for ALL NAT values — the single most consequential finding of the entire NAT sweep. Sparse attention changes KV cache contents by evicting middle tokens (TRISHAPE mode). This creates a fundamentally different attention landscape at long context that could either: (a) SHIFT the AR collapse boundary higher (favorable — draft model predictions align better with pruned KV cache), or (b) WORSEN the collapse (unfavorable — draft model expects tokens that were evicted). Either outcome directly informs DEC-01 (adaptive NAT), DEC-02 (speculative decoding collapse policy), and DEC-03 (context cap). Running Task 4.3b BEFORE locking those decisions avoids premature architectural commitment based on dense-attention-only data.
API confirmed: SchedulerConfig.use_sparse_attention + SparseAttentionConfig with SparseAttentionMode.TRISHAPE. Intel demonstrated 2.6× TTFT reduction at 32K on exact BlarAI hardware (Arc 140V, Qwen3-14B). OV 2026.0 compatible.
Pending scope review by SDO — see DEC-04.
Terminal gate. Blocked on all prior tasks completing. GATEWAY_HANDSHAKE_FAILED regression (Task 5 blocker) also not yet root-caused. See copilot-instructions.md Phase 5 directive.

verification.expected_output: f48ea78 docs: fixup LEDGER Entry 17 commit hash reference cc919fb feat(benchmark): Task 4.3 -- NAT sweep x context bands (production config) a1fed28 docs(adr-012): Draft model status EVALUATING to LOCKED

command.description: Confirm evidence artifact exists and shows SDO_DECISION_REQUIRED

verification.expected_output: Line matching: ### Entry 17 — Task 4.3: NAT Sweep × Context Bands
Line containing: Task 4.3: NAT varies by band — SDO decision pending
670 passed (or equivalent baseline count passing, 0 failures)

required_attachments.attachment: .github/copilot-instructions.md REASON: Governance rules, phase directives, interaction rules. SDO cannot operate without this. Phase 5 ACTIVE directive must be current.
docs/Task4.3_EXECUTION_REPORT.xml (this file) REASON: Primary handoff document. Contains all benchmark data, gate results, disposition, and pending SDO decisions.

docs/P5_TASK4_PRODUCTION_CONFIG_FEASIBILITY.md REASON: SDO needs the full task sequencing table (§0) and remaining task specs (§7 Task 4.3b, §8+ scopes) to update roadmap and generate next execution prompt.

docs/adrs/ADR-012-Qwen3-14B-Model-Selection-Speculative-Decoding.md REASON: SDO must update §2.2 parameter rows based on DEC-01/DEC-02/DEC-03 decisions, and may need to add a new §2.6 for context-length-adaptive NAT strategy if DEC-01 resolves to Option B.
docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md REASON: SDO needs current entry count (Entry 17 is latest) to assign correct entry number to next milestone (Entry 18 = Task 4.3b).

docs/P5_TASK4_SDO_HANDOFF.xml REASON: Current SDO session initialization document (v3.4). Contains full Task 4 scope, sub-session definitions, and measurement requirements. SDO should cross- reference Task 4.3 actual results against §5 expected outcomes defined therein. NOTE: Do NOT include in Execution Agent attachments per Attachment_Scope_Discipline rule.
